title: Canonical Redirects
user_story:
As a returning visitor\
I want to be able to access NODES that I have bookmarked a long time ago\
So I don't have to search for that information again just because the URL changed over time
description:
* the goal is to make sure that old bookmarks and links are still accessible, even after 10 or 20 years
* when the request uses the canonical URL no redirect is performed → status code 200
* when the target node does not exist (anymore) the request should be rejected → status code 404
* when the request uses a non-canonical URL then a redirect to the canonical version i performed → status code 301
* the following patterns need to be supported:
  * `/<node-id>` -> Short URLs for F1 Book
  * `/<short-node-id>` → MC 1.0 short URLs
  * `/<node-type>/<node-id>` -> unofficial shortcut for MC 2.0
  * `/<node-type>/<short_node-id>` → unofficial shortcut for MC 1.0
  * `/<node-title>__<short_node-id>` → official MC 1.0 URLs
* a short node ID has 1 to 6 digits (More Cars 1.0)
* a regular node ID has 8 digits (More Cars 2.0)
* the **canonical URL** always has the pattern `/<node-title>-<node-id>`
